metadata.analyst_notes: Structured 10 customer requirements into 14 FRs (7 P0, 4 P1, 3 P2) plus 6 domain-research-suggested FRs (P1/P2). Core demo flow: NOTAM intake with AI scoring -> impact analysis dashboard with map -> briefing generation. AI features use Amazon Bedrock (no mocking). Seed data for NOTAMs, routes, flights, airports. J-FOS integration is out of scope.

pain_points.description: 3 dispatchers manually monitor thousands of NOTAMs daily via 24h shift rotation through AFTN — unsustainable workload
NOTAMs are unstructured text with cryptic abbreviations — interpretation is slow and error-prone
Important NOTAMs must be manually written into J-FOS REF BOOK — time-consuming and risks omission
Delayed analysis of urgent NOTAMs creates operational disruption risk
Excessive time spent filtering thousands of routine NOTAMs to find operationally significant ones
Manual creation of crew briefing materials (Company NOTAM, DISP COMMENT) is labor-intensive
Highly skilled dispatchers spend time on low-value interpretation tasks instead of high-value decisions like route deviation

functional_requirements.title: LLM-based NOTAM importance scoring
functional_requirements.description: Use LLM (Amazon Bedrock) to analyze incoming NOTAMs and assign an importance score (0-1 probability) indicating likelihood of REF BOOK registration. Learn patterns from 3 years of historical REF BOOK data via RAG. Consider Q-Code, keywords, and historical patterns.
acceptance_criteria: Each NOTAM displays an importance score between 0.0 and 1.0
Score is generated by Amazon Bedrock LLM (not mocked)
NOTAMs are classified into importance levels: critical, high, medium, low, routine
NOTAM list table shows score with color-coded importance badge
Scoring completes within 10 seconds per NOTAM

functional_requirements.title: Q-Code based initial classification
functional_requirements.description: Parse NOTAM Q-line to extract Q-Code and apply rule-based initial classification before LLM analysis. Map Q-Codes to predefined importance categories (e.g., QMRLC=runway closed=high, QMAXX=aerodrome general=medium).
acceptance_criteria: Q-Code is extracted and displayed for every NOTAM
Q-Code description is shown in human-readable form (e.g., QMRLC -> Runway Closed)
Rule-based classification runs before LLM scoring
Q-Code lookup table is available as reference data

functional_requirements.title: Spatial and schedule-based comprehensive analysis
functional_requirements.description: Combine spatial context (airport runway count, location) and flight schedule data to compute comprehensive NOTAM importance. Example: runway closure at a 4-runway airport vs 1-runway airport yields different severity.
acceptance_criteria: Impact analysis considers airport runway count and facilities
Impact analysis cross-references active flight schedule
Analysis result shows contextual severity explanation
Bedrock LLM generates natural-language impact summary

functional_requirements.title: Flight and route auto-matching
functional_requirements.description: Extract spatial data (coordinates, radius, altitude) from NOTAM body, then cross-reference against airline routes (NAVBLUE waypoints) and flight schedule to automatically identify affected flights.
acceptance_criteria: System lists all affected flights for a given NOTAM
System lists all affected routes for a given NOTAM
Matching considers spatial overlap (NOTAM area vs route path)
Matching considers temporal overlap (NOTAM validity vs flight schedule)
Affected flight list shows flight number, route, departure time, and impact type

functional_requirements.title: Critical NOTAM auto-filtering
functional_requirements.description: Automatically filter thousands of NOTAMs to surface only those with operational impact. Apply configurable importance threshold to separate critical/important NOTAMs from routine ones.
acceptance_criteria: NOTAM list supports filtering by importance level (critical, high, medium, low, routine)
Default view shows only critical and high importance NOTAMs
Filter shows count of NOTAMs per importance level
Table supports sorting by importance score, time, airport, Q-Code
Filtered vs total ratio is displayed

functional_requirements.title: Route impact analysis dashboard with map
functional_requirements.description: Visual dashboard showing NOTAM impact across routes on an interactive map. Display NOTAM affected areas as circles/polygons, overlay airline routes as waypoint-connected lines, and highlight intersection zones.
acceptance_criteria: Interactive map displays NOTAM affected areas (circle with center + radius)
Map shows airline routes as polylines connecting waypoints
Intersection zones between NOTAM areas and routes are visually highlighted
Dashboard includes summary widgets: total active NOTAMs, critical count, affected routes count, affected flights count
Map supports zoom, pan, and click-to-view NOTAM detail
Route selection filters map to show only that route and its NOTAMs

functional_requirements.title: Dispatcher briefing document auto-generation
functional_requirements.description: AI generates dispatcher approval document summarizing analyzed critical NOTAMs. Includes NOTAM summary, impact assessment, and recommended actions.
acceptance_criteria: Dispatcher can trigger briefing generation for a selected flight
Generated briefing includes all relevant NOTAMs with AI summaries
Briefing content is generated by Amazon Bedrock (not mocked)
Briefing shows draft status and supports review/approve workflow
Generated document can be previewed in formatted view

functional_requirements.title: Crew briefing materials auto-generation
functional_requirements.description: AI generates crew briefing package including DISP COMMENT, Company NOTAM, and other briefing materials. Content is tailored for flight crew consumption.
acceptance_criteria: System generates DISP COMMENT for selected flight
System generates Company NOTAM document
Generated content uses appropriate aviation terminology
Dispatcher can edit generated content before approval
Multiple briefing types can be generated for the same flight

functional_requirements.title: Route deviation decision guidance
functional_requirements.description: AI suggests alternative routes when a NOTAM significantly impacts an active route. Example: suggest using route 98 instead of route 1 for ICN-BKK when airspace restriction affects route 1.
acceptance_criteria: When a high-impact NOTAM affects a route, system suggests alternate routes
Alternative route suggestions include reason and comparison (distance, time)
Suggestions are generated by Amazon Bedrock LLM
Dispatcher can view original vs alternative route on map
Decision guidance is provided as recommendation, not automatic action

functional_requirements.title: NOTAM-route/schedule matching algorithm
functional_requirements.description: Core algorithm that calculates spatial-temporal intersection between NOTAM affected areas and airline routes/schedules. Uses coordinate geometry to determine if a route segment passes through NOTAM-defined airspace volume.
acceptance_criteria: Algorithm calculates spatial overlap between NOTAM circle and route polyline
Algorithm considers altitude restrictions (lower/upper limits)
Algorithm considers temporal overlap (NOTAM validity period vs flight time)
Results include list of affected route segments with distance through affected area
Matching runs for all active NOTAMs against all scheduled flights

functional_requirements.title: REF BOOK management
functional_requirements.description: Digital REF BOOK where dispatchers can review AI-suggested entries and confirm/edit/register important NOTAMs. Replaces manual paper-based REF BOOK workflow.
acceptance_criteria: AI suggests NOTAMs for REF BOOK registration with confidence score
Dispatcher can confirm, edit, or reject AI suggestion
REF BOOK entries are searchable and filterable
Entry shows linked NOTAM details, registered by, registered at
Historical REF BOOK entries are viewable for reference

functional_requirements.title: NOTAM detail view with AI analysis
functional_requirements.description: Detailed view of a single NOTAM showing raw text, parsed fields, AI importance score, impact analysis, affected flights/routes, and AI-generated plain-language summary.
acceptance_criteria: Detail page shows raw NOTAM text and parsed structured fields side by side
AI importance score and classification are prominently displayed
Affected flights and routes are listed with links
AI-generated plain-language summary is shown
NOTAM location is shown on a mini-map

functional_requirements.title: Flight schedule overview
functional_requirements.description: Table view of airline flight schedule showing all scheduled flights with departure/arrival info, route assignment, and NOTAM impact status per flight.
acceptance_criteria: Flight list shows flight number, departure/arrival airports, times, and status
Each flight shows count of affecting NOTAMs with severity indicator
Table supports filtering by airport, route, date, and NOTAM impact status
Clicking a flight navigates to flight detail with full NOTAM impact analysis

functional_requirements.title: Shift handover report generation
functional_requirements.description: Auto-generate shift handover briefing summarizing new, changed, and critical NOTAMs since the start of the previous shift. Ensures continuity across 24h dispatcher rotation.
acceptance_criteria: System generates handover report with NOTAMs received/changed since configurable time window
Report categorizes NOTAMs by importance level
Report highlights new critical NOTAMs and status changes
Generated by Amazon Bedrock LLM
Dispatcher can view and export the report

functional_requirements.title: NOTAM plain-language Korean summary
functional_requirements.description: LLM generates human-readable Korean summary for each NOTAM, translating cryptic ICAO abbreviations and Q-Codes into clear operational language.
acceptance_criteria: Each NOTAM has an AI-generated Korean plain-language summary
Summary explains what the NOTAM means operationally
Summary is generated by Amazon Bedrock (not mocked)
Summary appears alongside raw NOTAM text in detail view

functional_requirements.title: Critical NOTAM real-time alert banner
functional_requirements.description: Display prominent alert banner/notification when a NOTAM exceeding the critical importance threshold is received. Ensures immediate dispatcher attention.
acceptance_criteria: Critical NOTAMs trigger a visible alert banner at the top of the page
Alert shows NOTAM summary, affected airport/route, and importance level
Alert persists until acknowledged by dispatcher
Alert links to full NOTAM detail view

functional_requirements.title: Dispatcher audit trail
functional_requirements.description: Log all dispatcher actions on NOTAMs (view, analyze, approve, register to REF BOOK, generate briefing) for regulatory compliance traceability.
acceptance_criteria: All dispatcher actions are logged with timestamp, user, action type, and target NOTAM
Audit log is viewable in a searchable table
Log entries are immutable (append-only)

functional_requirements.title: NOTAM change tracking (NOTAMR diff view)
functional_requirements.description: When a replacement NOTAM (NOTAMR) is received, show a diff view highlighting changes from the original NOTAM.
acceptance_criteria: Replacement NOTAMs (NOTAMR) show link to the original NOTAM
Diff view highlights added/removed/changed content
NOTAM history chain is navigable (original -> replacement -> cancellation)

functional_requirements.title: NOTAM expiry management
functional_requirements.description: Auto-manage NOTAM lifecycle with expiry countdown display and automatic archival of expired NOTAMs.
acceptance_criteria: NOTAMs show remaining time until expiry
NOTAMs expiring within configurable window are flagged
Expired NOTAMs are automatically moved to archived status
Table supports filtering by expiry status

non_functional_requirements.constraint: Simple login with dispatcher role — prototype uses mock auth (no Cognito). Single role: dispatcher.
Bedrock LLM scoring and summarization should complete within 15 seconds per NOTAM. Batch processing for multiple NOTAMs should run concurrently.
Dashboard with map and up to 500 active NOTAMs should render within 3 seconds. Map interactions (zoom, pan) should be smooth at 60fps.
All UI labels, navigation, and user-facing text in Korean. NOTAM raw text remains in English (ICAO standard). AI-generated summaries in Korean.
Standard security headers (CSP, X-Frame-Options, etc.) via Next.js middleware. Input validation with zod schemas on all API endpoints.
Cloudscape components provide built-in WCAG 2.1 AA compliance. Ensure proper ARIA labels on custom map components.
Prototype seeds with at least 50 sample NOTAMs, 10 routes, 30 flights, 15 airports to demonstrate realistic filtering and analysis scenarios.

personas.name: Kim Dispatcher
goals: Quickly identify operationally significant NOTAMs from thousands of daily messages
Generate accurate crew briefing materials in minimal time
Make informed route deviation decisions with AI assistance
Maintain complete REF BOOK records without manual transcription
Ensure smooth shift handover with comprehensive summaries
pain_points: Spends 70% of shift on routine NOTAM filtering and interpretation
Manual REF BOOK entry is tedious and error-prone
Crew briefing document creation takes 20-30 minutes per flight
Fears missing a critical NOTAM during high-volume periods
Shift handover relies on verbal communication — information gaps occur

personas.name: Park Team Lead
goals: Ensure 100% critical NOTAM coverage across all shifts
Reduce team overtime caused by manual NOTAM processing
Maintain regulatory compliance for NOTAM handling audit trail
pain_points: Cannot scale team beyond 3 dispatchers due to budget constraints
Quality varies across shifts depending on dispatcher experience
No centralized view of NOTAM impact across entire route network
Difficult to demonstrate operational improvements without data

sla_definitions.response_time: AI analysis per NOTAM < 15 seconds; dashboard load < 3 seconds
sla_definitions.availability: Not applicable for prototype — single-instance deployment
sla_definitions.notes: Prototype targets demo quality, not production SLA. Bedrock API latency is the primary performance variable.

assumptions: Prototype uses seed data instead of live AFTN feed — sample NOTAMs, routes, flights, airports are pre-loaded
REF BOOK historical data is provided as seed data (not real 3-year dataset)
NAVBLUE route data is represented by sample route data with realistic waypoints
J-FOS integration is not in scope — this is a standalone web application
Single user role (dispatcher) with mock authentication — no Cognito/SSO
Map visualization uses open-source library (Leaflet or MapLibre GL)
Boeing 737 is the only aircraft type — no aircraft-specific branching logic needed
AI features are powered by Amazon Bedrock Claude — actual LLM calls, not mocked
Importance score threshold for critical classification is configurable but defaults to 0.8
NOTAM coordinate system follows ICAO standard (degrees/minutes/seconds)
Korean language for all UI text; English for NOTAM raw text and code
out_of_scope: Real AFTN integration or live NOTAM feed consumption
J-FOS system integration or data synchronization
NAVBLUE API integration — using seed data instead
User management, roles, and permissions beyond single dispatcher role
Mobile responsive design — desktop-first for dispatch workstation
NOTAM origination (creating new NOTAMs) — system is read/analyze only
Production deployment, scaling, and high availability
Data persistence beyond in-memory/file-based seed data
SNOWTAM/ASHTAM/BIRDTAM special format handling (standard NOTAM only)
Flight plan filing or ATC communication
